---
title: "Labs Overview"
---

Ten kurs jest zaprojektowany jako seria krótkich laboratoriów. Każdy moduł ma tę samą topologię:

1. **Short Intuition:** jedno pojęcie, bez przeciążania formalizmem.
2. **Executable Code Block:** minimalny, kompletny skrypt Python/PyTorch.
3. **Hands-on Tasks & Solutions:** zadania i rozwijana odpowiedź do samokontroli.

| Lab | Fundamental question | Main tools |
|---:|---|---|
| 1 | How does PyTorch represent data and remember operations? | `torch.Tensor`, `autograd` |
| 2 | How does error flow through matrix multiplication? | raw tensor matmul |
| 3 | Which linear directions explain most variance? | `torch.linalg.svd` |
| 4 | Why is accuracy misleading under class skew? | precision, recall, `F_beta` |
| 5 | How can anomalies be isolated without modeling normality? | `IsolationForest` |
| 6 | How do we measure surprise and distribution mismatch? | entropy, KL |
| 7 | What does a bottleneck force a model to learn? | `nn.Module`, MSE |
| 8 | How can a model learn a smooth latent distribution? | VAE, ELBO |
| 9 | How does adversarial training create samples? | GAN, BCE |
| 10 | Why does Wasserstein training stabilize GANs? | gradient penalty |
| 11 | What changes when latent space becomes discrete? | VQ, codebook |

::: {.callout-note}
## Build rule
The website build does not execute the lab code. This keeps GitHub Pages deployment light while preserving full executable scripts for students.
:::
